A001.explanation: Deterministic weighted score 16.7/100 from revenue trend (+23.3% 3m), net cash flow (127,600), diversification (0.97), peer gap (-41.8%) and engagement recency.

methodology.model_family: Additive weighted scorecard · transparent per-feature attribution
methodology.trained_alternative: scikit-learn RandomForest (used when per-cohort training data is sufficient)
pipeline: Compute the advisor's Phase-5 feature snapshot from graph facts
Select the 5 risk-driver features for Revenue Decline Risk
Score each feature by its documented weight → points (clamped 0–100)
Sum the points and clamp the total to a 0–100 risk score
Map score → risk band; derive confidence from feature coverage
Persist the score with a full reasoning trace (feature → points → score)
methodology.fallback_reason: MODEL_CLIENT_MODE=deterministic: risk scoring is served by the scorecard

A001.explanation: XGBoost revenue-decline model: 29.2/100 risk, revenue-weighted across 6 households (base 29.6). Real TreeSHAP attribution; held-out ROC-AUC 0.7755.

methodology.model_family: Gradient-boosted trees (XGBoost) · household-level, revenue-weighted to advisor · TreeSHAP attribution
methodology.trained_alternative: iPerform Risk Scorecard (deterministic fallback when the model's quality gate is not met)
pipeline: Build leakage-safe features for each of the advisor's households as of the latest data month
Predict P(6-month revenue decline) per household with the trained XGBoost model
Aggregate to advisor: revenue-weighted mean probability → 0-100 risk score
Attribute with TreeSHAP: revenue-weighted mean SHAP per feature → additive signed points
Band score → severity; derive confidence from the prediction margin
Persist score + contributions + reasoning trace (same artifact chain as the scorecard)
methodology.score_formula: risk = 100 · Σ_h w_h·P(decline_h) / Σ_h w_h,   w_h = household trailing-6m revenue
methodology.caveats: Demo-scale synthetic-seeded data; n≈2.2K household-period samples from 360 households / 60 advisors. Metrics indicate the pipeline is real, not production accuracy.
